title: Syracuse Police Chief Joe Cecile will retire when Walsh’s term ends this year
summary: Syracuse Police Chief Joe Cecile will retire on December 31, 2025, when Mayor Ben Walsh's term ends. Cecile, who has served 40+ years in the SPD and became chief in 2022, publicly recommends that his successor be selected from within the department's ranks, signaling a leadership transition decision point for the incoming mayor.

political_signal.description: Outgoing chief signals institutional continuity preference by publicly advocating for internal successor selection, positioning this as a governance priority for next administration.

processing_notes: Article establishes clear timeline: retirement effective 12/31/2025, coinciding with mayoral transition
Chief's public recommendation for internal promotion may constrain successor selection process
No information on interim chief appointment or recruitment timeline in article
Consider linking to mayoral election cycle and police department organizational charts in context engine
